rulings.Case Title: PETER ST. GEME et al VS. CHEVY CHASE BANK FSB, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Capital One, N.A., Successor By Merger To Chevy Chase Bank'S Motion To Compel To Deposition Subpoena For Business Records
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 9, 2012, Line 3, DEFENDANT CAPITAL ONE, N.A., Successor By Merger To Chevy Chase Bank's Motion To Compel To Deposition Subpoena For Business Records. Pro Tem Judge Mark Schreiber, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Parties to appear with proposed orders. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: EVGENI LITVAK et al VS. YURI GALASHOV et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 9, 2012, Line 10, CROSS DEFENDANT YURI GALASHOV's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Overruled as to the first, second, third and fifth causes of action, and sustained, with 10 days leave to amend, as to the fourth cause of action. Cross-Complainant has pled sufficient facts to support her first, second, third and fifth causes of action, and it is not determinable from the face of the First Amended Cross-Complaint or judicially noticeable facts that these claims are barred by the doctrine of res judicata, the applicable statute of limitations or on the asserted jurisdictional grounds. For the fourth cause of action,Cross-Complainant is granted leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action only, if she can do so in good faith, to allege each of the elements for a breach of contract cause of action based on the alleged withdrawal of $ 18,000. (CACI no. 303.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ESPERANZA COTA ROMERO VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 9, 2012, Line 14, DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled as to the first cause of action, sustained without leave to amend as to the second cause of action, and sustained with leave to amend as to the third cause of action. Plaintiffs have standing. The negligence claim is adequately pled. The second cause of action fails to state facts sufficient to state an intentional infliction of emotional distress claim. Given the nature of the allegations, since Defendants' actions were directed at the decedent and did not occur in Plaintiffs' presence, it is unlikely leave to amend can cure the defect. The third cause of action fails to allege facts with sufficient particularity to constitute a fraud claim. Plaintiffs are granted leave to amend, if they can do so in good faith, to allege, with particularity, Defendants' intent to induce reliance on its misrepresentation and Plaintiffs' justifiable reliance. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: TARGET NATIONAL BANK VS. EH SAKULTANAPANICH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Estella Sakultanapanich'S Motion To Compel Further Reesponses To Request For Production Of Documents From Target National Bank, For Sanctions, And In The Alternative, For Preclusion
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 9, 2012, Line 21, Defendant Estella Sakultanapanich'S Motion To Compel Further Reesponses To Request For Production Of Documents From Target National Bank, For Sanctions, And In The Alternative, For Preclusion. The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Kane as follows: Granted as to Request for Production ("RFP") nos. 6, 7, 10 and denied as to RFP nos. 1, 2, 5 and sanctions. With respect to the requests for production, Plaintiff's supplemental responses and statement that all responsive documents have been produced address requests numbers 1, 2, 5 and therefore the request for further responses is denied. Defendant's request #6 for production of credit reports may lead to the discovery of admissible evidence and as Plaintiff's has not addressed this specific request, the Plaintiff is ordered to produce any responsive documents. Defendant's request #7 is granted to the extent that any such documents exist as this is relevant to the subject matter. In this regard, Defendant claims that while Plaintiff does not have a application (request #1), it has some documentation relating to the application as it was able to state the date the account was open. Defendant's request #10 is granted to the extent that any such documents exist as this is relevant to the subject matter. The court adds a due date of August 31, 2012 to serve supplemental responses and produce responsive documents. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: TARGET NATIONAL BANK VS. EH SAKULTANAPANICH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Estella Sakultanapanich'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories From Target National Bank, For Sanctions, And In The Alternative, For Preclusion
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 9, 2012, Line 21, Defendant Estella Sakultanapanich'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories From Target National Bank, For Sanctions, And In The Alternative, For Preclusion. The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Kane as follows: Granted, in part, and denied as to sanctions. Based on Plaintiff's supplemental responses, further responses to interrogatories 1 and 2 are denied. With respect to interrogatory 3, based on Plaintiff's opposition, if Plaintiff has produced all such documents it needs to state that in a further response. Plaintiff's objection to interrogatory 4 will be required as what counsel intends to produce at trial is protected from discovery and is subject to disclosure pursuant to CCP §96. Defendant's interrogatory #10 for list of collection agencies that Plaintiff has contacted regarding this claim may lead to the discovery of admissible evidence and as Plaintiff's has not addressed this specific request, the Plaintiff is ordered to respond to this request. The parties should be aware that failure to disclose the names of witnesses or documents may preclude their introduction at trial. (Do It Urself Moving and Storage, Inc. v. Brown, Leifer, Slatkin & Berns (1992) 7 Cal.App.4th 27, 36.) The court adds a due date of August 31, 2012 to serve supplemental responses. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: TARGET NATIONAL BANK VS. EH SAKULTANAPANICH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Estella Sakultanapanich'S Motion For Order To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories From Target National Bank, For Sanctions, And In The Alternative, For Preclusion
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 9, 2012, Line 21, Defendant Estella Sakultanapanich'S Motion For Order To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories From Target National Bank, For Sanctions, And In The Alternative, For Preclusion. The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Kane as follows: Granted, in part, and denied as to sanctions. The specific documents that are part of the agreement were not attached interrogatory 150.1, Defendant is not required to look through the entire production to determine what Plaintiff considers the agreement. Also the custodial information needs to be provided. Similarly with respect to interrogatory 150.4, Plaintiff needs to state if there were modifications and if so describe or attach the specific documents and provide the custodial information. The parties should be aware that failure to disclose the names of witnesses or documents may preclude their introduction at trial. (Do It Urself Moving and Storage, Inc. v. Brown, Leifer, Slatkin & Berns (1992) 7 Cal.App.4th 27, 36.) The court adds a due date of August 31, 2012 to serve supplemental responses. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 9, 2012, Line 20, DEFENDANT SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALs' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained with leave to amend. The first cause of action for general negligence cannot stand on these facts and should be subsumed within the second cause of action for medical malpractice. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: TARA GANANCE VS. CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 9, 2012, Line 20, DEFENDANT SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS' MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. The motion to strike punitive damages is granted. If Plaintiff wishes to allege a claim for punitive damages, he shall follow the procedure set forth in Cal. Civ. Pro. § 425.13. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
